result.stdout:
Toml Config Management Tool v1.0.3.0
Verbs [encrypt | decrypt | verify]
USAGE: toml-config-tool [VERB] [OPTIONS]

Example: toml-config-tool encrypt --auto -f config.toml -k .+Password.+ 
This will generate a new key and encrypt the config.toml file with it. all fields containing password will be encrypted

Use this tool to encrypt, decrypt and verify secrets in your toml files
Options:
  -f, --file=VALUE           input file name, can use wildcards or specify
                               multiple times.
  -k, --key=VALUE            config keys containing secret data. This in a
                               regex that should match properties to be
                               encrypted/decrypted. it defaults to any key
                               containing 'password' in the name
  -r, --recursive            when specified would search subfolders for files
  -m, --master_key=VALUE     the master key to use, defaults to the value
                               provided by 'MASTER_KEY' environment variable
  -a, --auto                 Will generate and use a new master key, the value
                               will be written to console.
  -h, --help                 show this message and exit
